reviews.review_text: Ive put hundreds of hours into this in the past. In its time it was the game we all drove home quick from work to play. Now its dated but im doing campains for old time sake and find myself going through it with a smile. Its still fun :)

The legendary RTS that defined a genre and still remains the gold standard for tactical combat.

The original Company of Heroes isn't just a classic; it’s a masterclass in game design that fundamentally changed how we think about real-time strategy. Before this game, most RTS titles were about gathering wood and gold to build a massive army. Relic Entertainment flipped that on its head, focusing instead on "territory control" and "tactical positioning." The result is a game that feels incredibly visceral and high-stakes. Every hedgerow, brick wall, and bombed-out building in Normandy matters. It turned the battlefield into a living, breathing puzzle where a single well-placed anti-tank gun or a daring flanking maneuver by a squad of paratroopers could turn the tide of an entire match.

Even decades later, the physics and destructibility in this game are more impressive than many modern titles. Seeing a Tiger tank crash through a stone wall or watching a mortar shell leave a permanent crater in the road isn't just visual flair—it’s a core part of the gameplay. The "Essence Engine" created a level of cinematic immersion that was unheard of in 2006, making every skirmish feel like a scene pulled straight out of Saving Private Ryan. The campaign, which follows Able Company through the liberation of Europe, remains one of the best-paced and most memorable narratives in the genre, serving as both a brilliant tutorial and a gripping war story.

While sequels have come and gone, there is a "purity" to the original Company of Heroes that keeps its community alive to this day. The balance between the factions—the versatile Americans and the defensive-heavy Wehrmacht—is legendary, leading to some of the most intense and competitive multiplayer matches in RTS history. It’s a game that demands your full attention; you can't just "blob" your units and hope for the best. You have to manage suppression, retreat at the right moment to save your veterans, and constantly adapt to a changing environment. It is a timeless masterpiece that every strategy fan needs to have in their library.

10/10 — I lost three squads trying to take a single fuel point, and I’d do it all over again. A true GOAT.

Great RTS, not too intense but not a cakewalk either. The campaigns are memorable and engaging, driven by high stakes and an intense atmosphere. PvP and PvE multiplayer games are still active too, which was a pleasant surprise.

Almost all cover can be destroyed which adds a new degree of tactics along with an element of narrative to each battle as it rages on. Sound design is awesome, and the musical stingers are well done. 

Base building isn't a huge part of the game, which allows you to focus more on your troops as you pick fights and move around. The AI can give you a proper challenge even on Hard, but Expert is where you might start to break into a cold sweat. 

Definitely a must-try if you're a fan of RTS, but it's also a very good introduction to the genre. Very cheap during Steam sales, and it'll run on weaker machines as well.
It a good player v player game. Run on low spec laptop. Recommend it.

i played this game long before around 2010 i think, its really really fun. i decided to purchase this game never regretted it. i tried the coh 2 but it feels like i need to snort some chems to play that one. This game is perfect for me. 140+ hours of fun and still going up. i hope that the mods will be fix since it adds alot of fun to the game. yes i use potato laptop that i decided to ressurect using linux mint.

It's a good game for what it is however terrible community and I will not recommend you playing with randoms when you're new because you will never be accepted to join any games and will never level up since only the diehard fans are still here since the beginning who are the ones left playing it, unless you can find a friend to join you then it's ok.

campaigns are decent but once you finish them, no replay value.

playing skirmish against AI, is a laughable cheez fest with it as Expert, it knows the roll of the die before it even rolls it and can be very frustrating.

Toxic community that lacks the concept of teamwork and only knows how to act like an elitist, even in the Skirmish game.

Imagine spending your manpower on an Observation Post so the rest of the team would have sufficient ammunition and fuel at the cost of having a small army, which in turn forced you to barricade the entire area with barbed wire and tank traps to prevent it from falling into enemy hands.

Only to have this bunch of selfish, entitled moron spamming the chat, telling you to use your only PAK that is holding the line to destroy a tank while refusing to give line of sight by using a sniper as reconnaissance. 

Imagine making it possible for them to mass-produce tanks, only to have your face spit on. Buy this game if you want to play the campaign, but stay away from multiplayer.
One of classic RTS games i played and love.  Spent 50+ hours to complete all the campaigns. I love everything about this game. NOW! let me try the multiplayers.. hope im not too late to start one

Its a good game.
a verity of units, vehicles, soldiers, even the air force have a role to play.
the campaigns are fun yet routed in history skirmish mode is cool too.
wish they added a little more like other fronts not just the west but the game is great.
Would recommend.  (the graphics are decent too.)

Lovely game, holds up pretty great. I would say that the positioning click could use some work namely for actually properly giving the dots proper positioning and not standing in front of cover, but its an old game so those are acceptable pathing issues.


Would recommend 100% get it even if not on sale.
I've been playing this game on/off since it's launch in 2011. YES! I recommend it to anyone that likes RTS topdown strategy games.
its too bad relic never took up after the original blitzkrieg mod.
This 20 year old game still works on the latest Panther Lake chip from Intel, just make sure your C++ Redistributable runtime libraries are up to date.
One of the best real time strategy games of all time. The Invasion of Normandy campaign is unparalleled. 20 years old and it still holds up.
Holds up the decade or so since i've played. Excellent gameplay and engaging story.
Works perfectly on my UHD i7th gen, 16GB RAM, 1080p Med-High settings and good frames, visually appealing, i love this game and its voicelines are ICONIC!!
A classic. I remember wanting to play this game since my childhood, and now that I have, it definitely surpassed my expectations.
Just a great classic! Still better than COH3. More depth and the factions are actually unique. Why did we get away from this formula?
With the original being this good I see no reason to play the other 2 games.

One of the best RTS so far... 

10/10 Will always play.
